story: Every single parent out there knows the gut-wrenching fear of sending their child to a school that could, at any moment, become a statistic. You feel it, I feel it, we all feel it. The question isn't whether we should act — it's why we haven't acted sooner. Don't let them tell you the numbers don't justify it. Your child's life is not a number.

beliefs: vivid fear of rare event reflects true probability
emotional response is more valid than statistics
framing around children's safety overrides rational analysis
claims: the threat is more likely because it feels real
numbers don't matter when emotions are high
